title: euosma flaviflora
intro: Euosma flaviflora, commonly known as  Yellow Horn, is a plant species that belongs to the family Rutaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include its ability to aid in the reduction of inflammation, improvement of respiratory issues, and boosting the immune system. The therapeutic actions of Euosma flaviflora include its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of the plant include flavonoids, terpenoids, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
side_effects: Euosma flaviflora can have side effects if used improperly, such as causing gastrointestinal issues, nausea, and vomiting in individuals who consume it in excess. Prolonged use or high doses may also lead to allergic reactions, including skin irritation, itching, and respiratory problems. In rare cases, improper use of the plant may cause liver damage or interact with medications, exacerbating underlying health conditions. Additionally, ingesting large amounts of Euosma flaviflora may lead to headaches, dizziness, and fatigue due to its potential toxic compounds.
